reasoning: As of late October 2025, there are only ~2 months remaining in the year. Despite earlier investor pressure regarding his focus and government roles, Musk has publicly committed to scaling back his political activities to focus on Tesla, and both he and the board have adamantly denied rumors of him stepping down. His new proposed compensation package further cements his long-term tenure. Given the short time horizon remaining in 2025, the chance of an abrupt departure (e.g., unexpected illness or extreme scandal) is very low, aligning closely with general CEO sudden departure base rates of ~1-2% for a two-month period.
